description: The bird was first found by homeowner John Ealy on April 25, and seen by him on April 26, and by John and me on April 27. The oriole is a brilliant yellow with black masked face, wide black bib, long bill, black tail, and a strong white wingbar. It was about as long as a starling but not nearly as chunky. John saw the bird just before I arrived on April 26. After it failed to return for 90 minutes, I played a hooded oriole song, which it responded to twice.  I heard it call "veek," which sounded close to the hooded oriole call on the Sibley app.
photographer: Photographer: Mark Obmascik, Mark Obmascik, John Ealy
